design_philosophy.core_loop: Whip-based combat and traversal through film-faithful platforming stages with occasional genre breaks (flying, mine cart, rafting sequences)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Action platformer challenge balanced against faithful movie adaptation—executing precise platforming and combat while experiencing Indiana Jones' three major adventures
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Indiana Jones through his greatest film adventures, using the iconic whip as primary tool for both combat and environmental traversal
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits heavily to Super Star Wars template (same engine) rather than innovating; prioritizes film fidelity and visual spectacle (Mode 7 effects, sprite work, faithful scenery recreation) over mechanical originality. Uses variety injection (different gameplay modes per stage) to sustain a 28-area single-experience progression through three movies

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system; player restarts from beginning of current area or last checkpoint upon death
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint-based within areas; some stages have mid-stage checkpoints to prevent excessive repetition
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited agency; hidden debug codes (level skip, level select, invincibility) exist but require knowledge and are lost upon reset. No difficulty selection menu documented

progression.upgrade_system: Occasional pickups: unlimited ammo gun (situational), limited grenades, whip as multi-purpose tool. No persistent upgrade system across levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Three movies (Raiders, Temple of Doom, Last Crusade) unlock sequentially via progression or password system. Raiders is the only playable movie at start
replayability_hooks: Level select code (requires game over to activate)
Three distinct movie campaigns with different environments and enemies
Genre variety per stage maintains novelty across 28 areas
Challenging execution encourages skill-based replays for speedrun/no-death runs

world_building_techniques: Text exposition (exact implementation unclear from sources)
Stage-by-stage faithful recreation of film locations and enemy types
Mode 7 visual effects and sprite work enhance cinematic atmosphere

notable_bosses: Walter Donovan (skeleton form—from Last Crusade climax)

danger_signals: High difficulty and 'frequent unavoidable hits' (per EGM reviewers) may frustrate casual players
Checkpoint system prevents soft-lock but brutal challenge could deter completion
environmental_storytelling: Stage design directly mirrors film locations (temples, deserts, grails, etc.)
Enemy types and placement reflect source material (Nazis, cult members, temple guardians)
Visual fidelity (color palette, sprite animation) reinforces authenticity to each film's aesthetic

sources_used: Wikipedia: Indiana Jones' Greatest Adventures (gameplay, release, reception sections)
The Cutting Room Floor: Indiana Jones' Greatest Adventures (developer credits, level select info, debugging material)
